title: Kefersteinia pusilla
taxonomy: Kefersteinia pusilla is classified as a species within the genus Kefersteinia, which falls under the family Orchidaceae. This family encompasses over 30,000 species of orchids, making it one of the largest and most diverse groups of flowering plants. The Orchidaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Kefersteinia pusilla belonging to the subfamily Vanilloideae. The subfamily Vanilloideae is characterized by its unique floral morphology and is commonly found in tropical regions of Asia, Africa, and the Americas. Kefersteinia pusilla is a small to medium-sized orchid species native to tropical Asia.

intro: Kefersteinia pusilla is a species of orchid native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Kefersteinia pusilla include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
